scores.materiality_reasoning: Recognition as a distinct species is a purely conceptual/taxonomic categorization.
scores.generality_reasoning: This is a specific intellectual classification used as a philosophical example.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Ethnicity and species-status are treated as permanent, inherent characteristics.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Greeks (Hellenes) is defined by blood and lineage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The author refers to 'most people in this country' at his own time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a widespread contemporary social convention as an observed fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being Greek does not inherently define one as a 'distinct species' in all systems.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Private fish ponds are tangible property and a source of food/wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a particular type of luxury infrastructure (fish preserves).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Held as long as one maintains the royal office or property rights.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Kingship is a structural status, often hereditary and very hard to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the existing king of Persia and known Egyptian structures.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author cites these as heard-of or seen factual examples of foreign practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a king does not definitionally include owning fish ponds.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Political power ('reins of the state') has direct material consequences for people's lives.
scores.generality_reasoning: Political power is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The power is stable for the duration of the 'statesman's' tenure.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Statesmanship is an acquired 'science' or occupation.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The dialogue discusses the nature of the statesman in contemporary society.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The text uses 'ought to' and describes an ideal classification/prescriptive role.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The rule merely states that the 'statesmanlike man' should provide 'statesmanlike' care.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Control over animal breeding and midwifery has direct economic/biological outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the professional domain of animal husbandry.
scores.persistence_reasoning: This role is a durable professional identity and vocational right.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Herdsman is an occupation acquired through training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes common contemporary agricultural practices.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Presented as a known fact: 'no one will ever raise such a contention'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Herding sheep does not analytically entail acting as a 'physician/midwife'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Music is a cultural practice with symbolic value and psychological effect on livestock.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific to the act of enlivening or soothing animals with music.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Held throughout the duration of the professional career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation requiring training and specialized equipment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes common contemporary pastoral life.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Presented as an observable reality of how herdsmen operate.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a herdsman does not naturally define one as a musician.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to the throne determines control over state resources and land.
scores.generality_reasoning: Succession is a broad category governing the transfer of total power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Settles the right to rule for life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Royal claimants are defined by birth/lineage in this context (Atreus).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The text explicitly refers to the mythological/heroic past of Atreus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the myth and its internal legal logic as a known narrative fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Divine tokens are not part of the definition of a 'claimant'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to education and breeding standards constitutes significant social capital/upbringing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Refers to a specific social status marker (shared upbringing).
scores.persistence_reasoning: One's breeding and education are permanent traits acquired in youth.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Statesmen here are defined by their current political role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to 'statesmen who now exist here'.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author observes this as a sociological characteristic of his peers.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a leader does not inherently mean having the same education as the subjects.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to rule is a foundational political/legal resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the broad authority of the 'kingly' man.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Held for the duration of the royal tenure.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group is defined by possessing 'the art' (knowledge).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses contemporary definitions of kingship.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: This is a philosophical proposal: 'may we not declare...'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: He defines the 'true king' as the one who rules voluntary subjects.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Power based on compulsion involves the physical control of subjects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Describes the general exercise of political power in a city.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Characteristic of the entire duration of a tyrannical regime.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Tyrant is a status acquired by seizing power.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a contemporary political reality known to Athenians.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: A mix of descriptive political theory and argumentation.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Defines a tyrant as one who uses compulsion.

scores.materiality_reasoning: State management involves control over laws, taxes, and warfare.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis: political sovereignty.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Typically held for long terms or life in 'royal classes'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'royal class' implies a structural, often hereditary division.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discussing contemporary claims to power in various states.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Investigating a 'scientific' understanding of an existing debate.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The 'royal class' is defined by its care of the state.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Advanced dialectical training/methodology is a significant intellectual resource/schooling.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a particular high-level educational method.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Training results in a permanent lifelong cognitive ability.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Pupils are in a changeable life-stage (learning).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary dialectical training practice in the Academy.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the factual objective of their current investigation.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a pupil does not automatically grant dialectical skill.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for kingship is a concrete legal right to supreme office.
scores.generality_reasoning: Right to hold the highest public office.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Kingship is the structural, lifelong head of state.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Priesthood is a structural legal/birth status in this text.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites contemporary Egyptian and Greek political structures.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a clear factual/legal requirement in Egypt and Greece.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Priesthood is logically separate from secular kingship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Performing national sacrifices is a high-status civic role with ritual authority.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the ritual domain of state religion.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Term of office is strictly annual ('chosen by lot').
scores.immutability_reasoning: Office-holder for a single year (King Archon).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the current Athenian Athenian King Archon.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explicitly cites a 'very plain' current Athenian practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: An Archon is not by definition the performer of sacrifices.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The power to execute or exile involves direct control over bodies and land.
scores.generality_reasoning: Extreme legal powers over life and death.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Expressed here as a single procedural act ('killing or banishing').
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by acquired expertise ('possessing science').
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: A theoretical proposal for contemporary government.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: This is an ideal philosophical proposal/wish (normative theory).
scores.tautology_reasoning: Scientific knowledge is not defined by the right to kill.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to rule without law grants absolute discretionary power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational axis of political authority.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Power held for as long as one is recognized as 'ruler'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status based on acquired scientific knowledge.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Investigating contemporary forms of government.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: A purely normative assertion of what is 'preeminently right'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being 'scientific' does not define an extra-legal right.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Vulnerability to the death penalty is a direct threat to bodily integrity.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad legal obligation to adhere to every law.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizen status and the threat of death are lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the 'second choice' (actual states) in the present.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports actual legal practice in contemporary 'non-ideal' cities.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not define death for every law violation.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Public audit (euthyna) has material consequences (fines/punishment).
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the procedure of annual office accountability.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Describes a single procedural moment at the end of the year.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Rulers here are annual office-holders.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the standard democratic audit system (euthyna).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a factual Athenian legal institution in detail.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Ruling does not analytically require a public trial after.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Eligibility for an archonship (political office) is a concrete legal right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to high executive and judicial power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies for the duration of the annual term (life stage of ruling).
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'rich' is a wealth bracket (durable but changeable).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Direct reference to contemporary Greek lotteries for office.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of contemporary political eligibility rules.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealth is not a part of the definition of an archon.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Serving as a judge grants power over legal outcomes and penalties.
scores.generality_reasoning: Judicial power is a broad structural resource.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Brief episode (serving for specific court sessions).
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'rich' are a durable wealth group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary selection for law courts.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a fact of how contemporary courts are composed.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being rich does not definitionally include being a judge.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Subjecting someone to 'extreme penalties' (death) is a material threat.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to cases of 'investigating contrary to the rules'.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Death is a permanent resource loss.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by an action ('found to be investigating').
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary laws against impiety/science (e.g. Socrates).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports existing laws/norms that penalize non-traditional study.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A scholar/investigator is not defined by their liability to death.
